commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=82f8ba9a474f1163e4abd1cea3e803dbc64d38b0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes embedded-agent model-call diagnostics to count high-frequency stream deltas from incremental `delta` bytes, strips growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting, adds focused regression tests, updates logging docs, and includes two small type-only cleanup commits.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +101, Docs +2. Total +141 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main serializes every streamed chunk with `JSON.stringify`, while delta event types carry an incremental `delta` plus a growing `partial` assistant message. I did not run the repro in this read-only review, but the PR adds a focused regression test that would exercise the path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is protected by a maintainer label and changes an exposed diagnostic metric contract, so the next action is maintainer compatibility review plus the narrow OTEL metadata fix rather than automated cleanup.

target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots and keeps streams alive if byte inspection throws.
- Document the bounded `responseStreamBytes` semantics for high-frequency streamed deltas.
- Include two small current-main gate unblockers hit by this changed lane: type the exec approval mock allowlist result, and remove a redundant `unknown | undefined` schema-cache annotation.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts --reporter=dot` (84 tests passed after final rebase on `82f8ba9a474`)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/openai-tool-schema.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean, no accepted/actionable findings after final rebase)
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4605e66e6f2f`, run `run_e94bc87a3cea`, base `b7e9272dbec`, head `013fb59dc0e`: exit 0. Final branch was then cleanly rebased to `82f8ba9a474` on `31169ff3b43`; focused tests, diff check, file-level oxlint, and autoreview were rerun after that rebase.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree plus AWS Crabbox changed gate through `core`, `coreTests`, and `docs` lanes.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, including assertions that `text_delta` accounting does not call `partial.toJSON` and that opaque diagnostic chunks do not break stream delivery; Crabbox run `run_e94bc87a3cea` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: `responseStreamBytes` is computed from incremental delta bytes for high-frequency stream deltas and from snapshotless chunks for other partial-bearing stream events.
What was not tested: a live long-running local-model session; the fix is validated through the diagnostics stream accounting path and changed-gate proof.
target.author: vincentkoc
